summary: Applied ADR 0164 live on docker-runtime-lv3 by fixing the API gateway runtime packaging so the shared circuit-breaker-enabled gateway stack can import the repo platform package, the repo config tree, and the repo scripts tree without crashing at startup.

verification.observed: `curl http://127.0.0.1:8083/healthz` returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` with `{"status":"ok"}` after the rebuilt container started with the repo `platform`, `config`, and `scripts` surfaces available.

verification.check: Authenticated API surface is serving requests
verification.observed: `curl http://127.0.0.1:8083/v1/health` returned `401 {"detail":"missing bearer token"}`, which confirms the live gateway is routing authenticated API paths instead of crashing during startup.

verification.check: Runtime mounts include repo config for packaged platform modules
verification.observed: `docker inspect api-gateway` showed `/config /opt/api-gateway/config`, `/app/config /opt/api-gateway/config`, and `/data /opt/api-gateway/data`, matching the runtime packaging needed for circuit policy and ledger/event taxonomy lookups.

notes: The first controller-side `ansible-playbook playbooks/api-gateway.yml` attempt on current `main` failed because the inventory still points `proxmox_florin` at the stale Tailscale IP `100.118.189.95`; the live converge used the working Proxmox jump path through `ops@100.64.0.1` instead.

Repeated full-role reruns over the jump path dropped SSH during the large recursive `platform/` sync, so the final live apply completed by streaming the exact repo-managed gateway files from the worktree into `/opt/api-gateway/service` and rebuilding the compose-managed `api-gateway` container in place.

The live runtime packaging fix had three parts: resolve repo root correctly inside the container layout, expose the repo config tree at both `/config` and `/app/config`, and bake the full repo `scripts/` tree into the image so transitive imports such as `maintenance_window_tool` and `mutation_audit` resolve at startup.
